label: Container / Kubernetes Security Engineer
description: Secures containerized workloads and Kubernetes clusters — pod security, RBAC design, network policies, runtime security, image scanning, and service mesh security.

paths.name: CNCF Track
paths.rationale: CNCF CKS is the only widely recognized Kubernetes security certification. Performance-based exam in real clusters proves actual K8s security skill.
pros: CKS required by name in most Kubernetes security roles
Performance-based exam in real clusters — proves actual K8s security skill
KCSA provides accessible entry point without CKA prerequisite
Cloud provider agnostic — applies across EKS, AKS, GKE
cons: CKS requires active CKA certification as prerequisite — adds time and cost
Kubernetes evolves rapidly — certification content updates with each release
Requires hands-on cluster experience — cannot be studied theoretically

prerequisites.experience: CKA (Kubernetes Administrator) required before CKS. Practical K8s engineering experience essential.

cert_sequence.notes: Kubernetes and Cloud Native Security Associate — foundational K8s security concepts without the CKA prerequisite. Good orientation to the security landscape. Killer.sh simulator provides excellent hands-on preparation.

Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist — the definitive K8s security credential. 2-hour performance-based exam in real Kubernetes environments. Cluster hardening, system hardening, microservice vulnerabilities, supply chain security, monitoring and runtime security. Practice daily in a kubeadm-provisioned cluster — not just minikube.

paths.name: DevSecOps + Container Track
paths.rationale: For security engineers who combine container security with DevSecOps pipeline work — the most common combined role in cloud-native organizations.
pros: CDP + CKS covers the full DevSecOps and container security scope
Strong combination for platform security engineer roles
cons: Long timeline — both CDP and CKS require significant preparation

prerequisites.experience: DevOps or platform engineering background beneficial.

cert_sequence.notes: Container and K8s security foundation before pipeline integration.
Practical DevSecOps Professional — adds CI/CD pipeline security context. How containers get built and deployed is as important as how they run securely.

Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist — container runtime security capstone. With CDP pipeline context and KCSA foundations, CKS focuses on cluster runtime security rather than starting from scratch.
